name: Sendero del Sabinar del Arlanza (PR-BU-78) — circular desde Espinosa de Cervera
region_intro: Hiking around Espinosa De Cervera offers access to a diverse natural environment within the province of Burgos, Spain. The area is situated at an altitude of approximately 1,000 meters, characterized by Mediterranean vegetation including juniper, oak, and holm oak forests. The landscape features dryland crops, pastures, and river valleys, with the Esgueva River originating within the municipal boundaries. Peña Alta, at 1,070 meters, provides elevated views of the surrounding natural park. Best hiking trails around Espinosa De Cervera The most popular hiking route is Sendero de las Loberas (PRC-BU-69) — circular desde Caleruega, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route explores the local terrain from Caleruega. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sendero del Sabinar del Arlanza (PR-BU-78) — circular desde Espinosa de Cervera, a moderate 9.2 miles (14.7 km) path. This route offers a circular journey through the notable juniper forests of the Arlanza. Local hikers also love the Sabinar del Arlanza Trail (PR-BU 78), a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through juniper woodlands, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Espinosa De Cervera is defined by juniper forests, oak woodlands, and river valleys, with routes offering options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Espinosa De Cervera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Espinosa De Cervera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Espinosa de Cervera?
A: There are nearly 30 hiking routes around Espinosa de Cervera, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 7 easy trails, 19 moderate routes, and 3 more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Espinosa de Cervera?
A: The landscape around Espinosa de Cervera is characterized by Mediterranean vegetation, including extensive juniper forests (sabinares), oak woodlands (robledales), and holm oak groves (encinas). You'll also find dryland crops, vast pastures, and river valleys, with the Esgueva River originating in the area. Some trails may lead through narrow gorges and offer views of high mountains.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, Espinosa de Cervera offers several circular hiking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292500300/sendero-del-sabinar-del-arlanza-pr-bu-78-circular-desde-espinosa-de-cervera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Sabinar del Arlanza (PR-BU-78)&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through notable juniper forests. Another well-regarded circular trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131478381/sendero-de-las-loberas-prc-bu-69-circular-desde-caleruega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de las Loberas (PRC-BU-69)&lt;/a&gt;, starting from Caleruega.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Hikers can discover a variety of natural monuments and viewpoints. Notable features include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3568119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Peña Alta (1078 m)&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4997641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peña de Carazo&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3632504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinarejos Spring&lt;/a&gt; and ancient holm oaks, some possibly thousands of years old.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to discover on the trails?
A: Yes, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6062888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Churrión Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity of Espinosa de Cervera. It's a natural highlight worth seeking out during your hike.

Q: Is Espinosa de Cervera suitable for family hikes?
A: Espinosa de Cervera offers 7 easy trails, which are generally suitable for families. These routes provide a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful juniper and oak forests without significant elevation changes, making them enjoyable for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails are not detailed, the natural environment of Espinosa de Cervera, with its extensive forests and open pastures, is generally welcoming for dogs. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to follow local signage.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Espinosa de Cervera?
A: The region experiences a continental Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking, offering mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, especially at lower altitudes, while winter brings colder temperatures and potential for snow in higher areas like the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: Are there specific parking areas for hikers?
A: While specific hiker parking areas are not explicitly detailed, Espinosa de Cervera is a small village, and parking is generally available within or at the edges of the village. For routes starting directly from the village, you can typically find suitable parking. For trails further afield, look for designated parking at trailheads or near points of interest.

Q: Do I need any permits to hike in Espinosa de Cervera?
A: Generally, permits are not required for hiking on the established trails around Espinosa de Cervera. The area is part of the Espacio Natural de los Sabinares del Arlanza, and while it's a protected natural space, access for hiking is usually unrestricted. Always respect local regulations and private property.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails here?
A: The trails in Espinosa de Cervera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the quiet beauty of the juniper and oak woodlands, the panoramic views from elevated points like Peña Alta, and the sense of immersion in a diverse natural environment.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are 3 difficult routes available. One such demanding trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292908981/alto-de-la-cabeza-trail-prc-bu-77" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabeza Trail (PRC-BU 77)&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes and covers a longer distance, providing a rewarding experience.
tips: From this place the tour begins.
When you take the Camino de la Serna and come from Espinosa de Cervera, you will see the Ermita de San Ginés, which is located at the top of a hill.
Impressive views, with a beautiful wooden cross.
The hermitage is located in the Lomas de Cervera area, below Picacho and Pico Castro. An old early medieval town named “Talamanquiella” was probably located in this place.
A good slope with good views of the natural park from the top.
